title: NGE based on Sephiroth Tree of Life
display_title: NGE based on Sephiroth Tree of Life
wikitext:
[]: NGE based on Sephiroth Tree of Life?             Quote Post by xfactor30seconds » Wed Jul 08, 2009 8:28 pm http://www.evamonkey.com/writings_chung01.htm I first found this idea on evamonkey.com on the link above. I thought the idea was pretty well justified in the essay and I wanted to see if there were any flaws I didn't see or if anyone has anything to add.

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Wed Jul 08, 2009 11:55 pm To be honest I never rally looked into the sephiroth tree before. I knew it was used but I didn't really think about it. Obviously Anno used it heavily in the creation of characters

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 09, 2009 12:15 am Wellllll, if you want to go by SToL symbolism in the actual show... Reichu in some post from 2007 wrote: On a totally random "isn't that nifty?" note, the series associates the "Ikari Family" with the central pillar of the SToL: Gendo is Kether, Yui/EVA-01 is Tiphareth, Shinji is Yesod, and Rei is Malkuth. (SEE: Shinji in Gendo's office, #19; SToL in the Sky, #26'; Sephirotic Sauna, #25')

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Thu Jul 09, 2009 3:59 am The qabbalah is something I doubt anno read into at all other than its key themes. I strongly doubt he had any interest in forming characters based on sephirah when he seemed to be having plenty of fun molding them around personality disorders.

[]:             Quote Post by xfactor30seconds » Thu Jul 09, 2009 7:01 am it seemed like Anno wouldve gone through a lot of trouble to create the series. I thought maybe he may have loosly based it on that. The person who wrote the essay thing also seemed to have some good points.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 09, 2009 7:24 am The fact that the cited essay doesn't hit on any of the Character-Sephiroth associations that visually appear in the show ought to speak for itself...

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Thu Jul 09, 2009 11:36 pm does it? what does it say?

[]:             Quote Post by holdmelikeonnaboo » Mon Jul 13, 2009 1:52 am I think it is easy to compare something like that to Evangelion because the themes in both works are so broad and universal. It doesn't necessarily mean Evangelion was inspired by that though. But who knows

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Mon Jul 13, 2009 11:40 am I'm not sure I agree that there's a detailed correspondance between the SToL and characters. Delightful essay, though. Not meaning to be mean-spirited, but many a time I've found ZOMG qualities in horoscopes and fortune cookies...but kinda doubt these were linked to RL.
